item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The image that was used in this article has been replaced with the following image, which is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0):

Source: Wikimedia Commons
License: CC BY-SA 3.0
Title: Pocket Watch.jpg
Author: Horst Günther Burkhardt III
Release year: 2008

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: These look good to me.

item.title: Junior Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk Team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

This is a Bright Family skip, and a lot of connections to other Bright Family things could be made.

D-0991: NO ONE ASKED THEM. They just took it. They take it every time. Even when the roots run deep… deep deep deep into the bedrock, so deep that they crack it and it reforms around them. One of… one does all the bleeding. All the bleeding for all of them. And another one does all the living. And they have done. They do all the bleeding and all the living… and now. Now one more. And he does the dying.

This line makes reference to the three Bright Sibling skips, and the two that are not already crosslinked can be referenced. The third is crosslinked later, and crosslinking now would spoil the SCP.

D-0991: NO ONE ASKED THEM. They just took it. They take it every time. Even when the roots run deep… deep deep deep into the bedrock, so deep that they crack it and it reforms around them. One of… one does all the bleeding. All the bleeding for all of them. And another one does all the living. And they have done. They do all the bleeding and all the living… and now. Now one more. And he does the dying.

To further solidify these connections,we can link the explicit mention of Doctor Bright to a new, good tale about him, Major Tom. (The paragraph below also makes explicit mention of an SCP by number, and I would be remiss to not suggest a crosslink.)

Following this interview, SCP-076-2 breached containment at Site-19, killing an unreleased number of personnel and wounding ██ others. At the time of breach, Dr. Jack Bright was transporting object EO-963 by hand, and was among the first KIB (killed in breach). Approximately █ days later, D1-113, tasked to clear the area of rubble, discovered SCP-963 in the wreckage. Please refer to Interview Log 963 for more detail. Subsequent interrogation of the acting Navigator confirms that SCP-2799-4 depicted the face of Dr. Bright.

To:

Following this interview, SCP-076-2 breached containment at Site-19, killing an unreleased number of personnel and wounding ██ others. At the time of breach, Dr. Jack Bright was transporting object EO-963 by hand, and was among the first KIB (killed in breach). Approximately █ days later, D1-113, tasked to clear the area of rubble, discovered SCP-963 in the wreckage. Please refer to Interview Log 963 for more detail. Subsequent interrogation of the acting Navigator confirms that SCP-2799-4 depicted the face of Dr. Bright.

Finally, the last addendum is from an unmarked O5 with a two digit designation. Since this is a Bright-skip… that could easily be Twelve. And if it's Twelve, we can link to one of the tales about that deals with O5-12 being a Bring.

Changing this:

In a related decision, Dr. Jack Bright will retain his status as a Foundation employee, and all privileges associated therewith. No Special Containment Procedures will be enacted for his person. SCP-963 designates the object containing his consciousness only, and not the consciousness itself.

- O5-██

To this:

In a related decision, Dr. Jack Bright will retain his status as a Foundation employee, and all privileges associated therewith. No Special Containment Procedures will be enacted for his person. SCP-963 designates the object containing his consciousness only, and not the consciousness itself.

- O5-12

If anyone has any other ideas, feel free to suggest them below.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not big on this one. Mostly, it just feels like an attempt to piggyback on SCP-963.

TECHNICALLY it can work as stated, as there's nothing inherently contradictory in the timeline, but…feels a bit ham-handed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this. A whole heckuva a lot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Argh, disregard. Wrong thread.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Blah, why.
At first i wanted to upvote it, solely for this mental image
And then, I was treated to a poorly understandable log (well, maybe I'm just not clever for deciphering this sort of shit), a Bright origin story, and a decision to phase it out.. why?
At worst, this was a nonsensical coincidence .. at best it predicted an anomaly forming on-site and it was definitely not responsible for Bright's creation or anything else (and definitely not for the breach).
I mean, I could see this being O5-6's attempt to cover his anomalous ass… but I hardly remembered he's Bright's relative, the number isn't stated in the article, and overall, it doesn't work the idea enough.

Ultimately, a downvote because I am disappoint.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I got all three Bright family references at once as soon as I hit "Bring her back, and nothing happens". I had been suspicious of it before, but that one was the one I solidly remembered the O5 Bright guy getting reprimanded massively for, and the "un-person" lines tied it all together.
And I was like "Damnit Bright"
and then I was like "wait bright didnt write this, i hope you guys got approval first"
To be honest the medallion things actually reminded me of Bright's medallion and it's redacted artificial counterpart already, so it wasn't a hard jump.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm very much in two minds about this. On the one hand, I enjoyed it once I got the connection to 963 and his story, though I definitely didn't get the link to the other two; I thought it all referred to 963 (living many times, bleeding a lot, and obviously dying a lot) and if I enjoyed it I feel I should upvote.

On the other hand, I certainly wouldn't enjoy it if I hadn't got the connection. The object itself is really rather bland, and only has interest because it relates to another SCP, which is not something I'm wholly comfortable with. It feels a little like it's hijacking the success of another.

No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah… okay, thanks for the clarification. I suppose this SCP object would be considered Thaumiel if that's how it is used nowadays. However, I would still love to see more about this SCP in action. For some reason it reminds of Captain Jack's own compass from the movie The Pirates of the Caribbean, which I think is pretty cool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: From what I've seen it used in, Thaumiel refers to an SCP that is integral to the Foundation's mission. Requitefahrenheit's definition is how it was initially used, but not how it's used nowadays.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aye, Bright tends to bury the lede pretty deep in his articles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My understanding is thaumiel is anti Keter. That is, used to contain Keter objects when it itself is anomalous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this SCP, but I do have a few problems with it… well namely two.

The first problem that I find with this SCP is that it's thaumiel. From what I understand thaumiel is meant to be like super-keter. Correct/forgive me if I'm wrong, but if my understanding is correct from what I read, the use of this SCP was discontinued, therefore I don't believe its object class should be thaumiel seeing as it poses no threat unless it is in use. Once again correct/forgive me if I'm wrong.

The second problem I have is that this only has one example of its use explained, which in this case is a popular SCP article of a popular avatar of the site. I feel like more of examples of its use would improve the article because it would help to take away from the whole "oh they only referenced a popular article to get up-votes" and because I feel like it tell more of a story about itself. (especially to those who are new to the site and would not get the reference to Dr. Bright's "family" which I will admit I didn't get right away either)

Now don't get me wrong I like this SCP as is, but I can't see myself up-voting it until it has more content about the SCP itself. So far it's a no vote from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this. I think some people have those preconceived notions of what "should" and what "shouldn't" be a SCP, and it gets in the way of them enjoying a good story. I think this is a good story. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is honestly the first I've ever heard of these skips being related in any way.

Pretty neat idea, considering.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I suppose that actually having the CLICK HERE FOR ACCESS whatnot would be kind of silly if it appeared on all of them. Save that it appears on some other articles, and… well, no canon, I guess.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, she was dead, but was reanimated. I admit it is a little tenuous but hallucinations aren't always the most solid things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm referring to the fact that, as someone who had a hand in its creation, I probably should not vote on it, even if I didn't literally write and post the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I usually feel kinda weird about SCPs whose interest comes only from their relation to other SCPs. The SCP is kinda bland, it's the story that comes after that is what's interesting, and that is only tangentially related to the SCP at the beginning.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ SPOILERS
- spoilers?

The full set, if I remember IRL!Bright's headcanon, is O5-6 (aka Cowboy), SCP-321, SCP-590, and of course SCP-963. Assuming the three Bright skips are the ones referenced in the interview, I think we can safely assume that 590 is the one doing the bleeding, 963 is explicitly the one dying, but I don't entirely understand the notion that 321's fate is "living" for all of them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Who's the third member of the bright family?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good fucking crossreference is this cliche!

So why do I like it so much?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, speaking of that, the link to Scantron's seed idea can be found here. Sorry we didn't mention it sooner, buddy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now that I know it, crazy talk is three times creeper. I'm upvoting just because of it (because I share TL333s' cheesy feeling).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If they're intrinsic to Foundation secrets, shouldn't they all have huge ACCESS DENIED warning signs on them?

They probably do, for lower-level employees. Imagine that you are someone cleared to work on a 2799-related project, and you'll see why we're able to see this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The crazy talk is about the Bright family.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's… not the conflict of interest he's talking about. At all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is one of the hardest upvotes I've ever settled upon, so I'll go through my thought processes.

The first, of course was, "Oh, great, everyone's doing Thaumiel now" but it's just Roget, so I guess that's okay I guess. (Why do we even keep records on Thaumiel objects, though? If they're intrinsic to Foundation secrets, shouldn't they all have huge ACCESS DENIED warning signs on them?)

Second was some appreciation for the image, the theme, and "Foundation Thaumaturgists", because seriously, wtf.

Third was a certain disdain for the Foundation creating a skip that finds other skips. This was mitigated entirely by the project being retired. (And I do like the old containment procedures.)

I was a little iffy on the interview, because it starts off like "standard crazy people talk", but there does seem to be something going on and it gets pretty niftily creepy by the end. Having internal logic is key to making crazy people talk work.

What's most conflicting, though, is the final part. I mean, having this be the thing that predicts/causes Dr. Bright's bonding with 963 is extremely cheesy, not to mention one of those "Do we even do this anymore?" moments. The only way I can think to put it is it feels Series I, and not in a good way. (Also wow, I hadn't upvoted 963, wtf.) Plus, I think this could straight-up exist without that part and be just fine.

So I'm actually not sure why I upvoted now, but I'll leave it to avoid seeming contrarian.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't find the article itself interesting, so the crossreferences really feel awkward, and I cannot support Senior Staff Author Avatar inserts in mainlist articles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The conflict of interest in the O5 Council has been alluded to many times before in pieces which involve Dr Bright, and it's in part of my own headcanon. Mind you, I try to make my headcanon as inclusive as possible, but I actually like the idea that the Foundation isn't as cold as it thinks it is, even in the upper echelons of the organization.

This is also crosslinking done in a matter doesn't seem gratuitous at all, and tells an interesting story as a whole. Thus, I've upvoted it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad to see y'all liked my idea enough to run with it. I like it, but due to the conflict of interest, I'll have to no-vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We already know it's cool and exciting, and you can, too!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A collaboration between FortuneFavorsBold and I! Hopefully it is cool and exciting!
